items.title: Stop Overpaying for EBS I The Keys to AWS Optimization | S16 E3

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this livestream is a practical deep dive into Amazon EBS cost optimization covering how to identify overprovisioned volumes, right-size storage, and leverage newer EBS volume types to align performance with cost. It also covers removing waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes, demonstrates monitoring tools, and shares actionable strategies to reduce EBS spending without sacrificing performance.
bullets: Identify and right-size overprovisioned EBS volumes
Use latest EBS volume types to match performance needs and cost efficiency
Eliminate waste from unused snapshots and outdated volumes
Demonstrations of monitoring tools and real-world optimization examples
Actionable strategies to cut EBS spending without degrading performance
takeaways: Regularly audit and right-size storage to reduce recurring wasted spend
Migrating to appropriate/newer EBS volume types can improve cost-per-performance
Implement snapshot lifecycle and retire outdated volumes to eliminate silent costs
Use monitoring tools and real-world metrics to guide optimization decisions

items.title: Let's talk about valuations ft. Tim Deal

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Valuation is getting a tech and AI glow-up. PwC outlines how it helps clients boost efficiency, quality and consistency at every step of the valuation process.
bullets: Valuations are being upgraded with technology and AI.
PwC demonstrates how these tools improve efficiency, quality, and consistency.
The enhancements apply across the entire valuation process (“every step of the way”).
Video title features Tim Deal and is from PwC US.
Description emphasizes AI, innovation, and business tech.
takeaways: Tech- and AI-driven valuations aim to increase efficiency, which can speed valuation workflows for grid and data center decisions.
Improved quality and consistency in valuations can provide more reliable inputs when assessing grid or data center investments.
A standardized, tech-enabled valuation process can help compare sites and scenarios more consistently for capital planning.

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Evan Bates shares why the future of figure skating has never looked brighter. The Team USA post conveys optimism about the sport and directs viewers to follow Team USA across its social channels and website.
bullets: Evan Bates praises the next generation of figure skaters.
Describes the future of figure skating as "never looked brighter."
Includes the #WinterOlympics hashtag.
Calls to action: subscribe to Team USA on YouTube and follow social channels.
Provides links to Team USA website and multiple social platforms.
takeaways: Olympics-related content can drive spikes in streaming and social traffic—ensure capacity and load balancing for affiliated media assets.
Prioritize CDN caching and video delivery optimization for Team USA video content to reduce latency during peak interest.
Monitor analytics around calls-to-action (YouTube subscriptions, social link clicks) to scale authentication and API services as needed.
Maintain high availability for Team USA web properties and media hosting during Winter Olympics activity windows.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb properties and spent three years making weekend commutes from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself. He added more cabins over time, and as of September 2025 the property has generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015: purchased a five-acre property in upstate New York to develop Airbnb rentals.
Built the initial A-frame cabin himself over three years, commuting on weekends from Connecticut.
Expanded the site with additional cabins after the first build.
By September 2025 the property had brought in more than $2.1 million in revenue.
takeaways: Significant revenue growth from rural short-term rentals indicates potential demand for reliable power and communications in tourist-focused developments.
Phased, owner-driven expansion can lead to incremental and sometimes unpredictable increases in local load—flexible capacity planning is useful.
Remote, decentralized operations highlight the value of remote monitoring, maintenance access, and resilient backup power options for distributed sites.
Developments that scale from single properties to multi-unit rentals may become important customers for distributed energy, backup solutions, and connectivity services.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description, KPMG’s podcast episode explains how operational changes—like moving production, distribution or teams—should be assessed as restructurings from the perspective of transfer pricing and valuations. The hosts outline how to recognise significant changes (including EBIT analysis), document restructurings timely, meet reporting obligations (TPR, MDR), and avoid common valuation mistakes using practical examples and a checklist.
bullets: How to identify a restructuring in transfer pricing terms and when an operational change is material.
Importance of analysing EBIT and changes in functional profile to assess impact.
Prepare thorough documentation early to reduce future compliance risk.
Reporting obligations highlighted: Local Transfer Pricing Reports (TPR) and Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR).
Role of valuations, typical errors, and examples (moving production/distribution, staff or contracts).
takeaways: Before relocating capacity or teams, assess transfer-pricing and valuation implications to justify arm’s-length outcomes.
Maintain timely documentation and be prepared for TPR and MDR filings to avoid retroactive disputes.
Quantify EBIT and functional changes after restructurings to support pricing and tax positions with valuation evidence.
Use a structured checklist (as recommended) to capture steps, responsibilities and common pitfalls during transitions.
